Title: A single bacterial genus maintains root growth in a complex microbiome

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced specifically under root growth inhibition conditions caused by RGI-inducing bacteria or Variovorax drop-out community
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Colonization with Arthrobacter CL28; Variovorax drop-out synthetic community; suppression by Variovorax CL14/full synthetic community
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker of elevated auxin response during bacterially induced root growth inhibition
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value in this paper; useful as a biomarker for auxin overload/root growth inhibition status
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned among 18 RGI-induced plant genes; not directly manipulated as a focal experimental gene controlling nutrient metabolite flux
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root apex/elongation zone context inferred from auxin-responsive program
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.2 was the only overlapping RGI-induced gene explicitly identified by name. 2. It encodes an IAA-amido synthetase associated with late auxin responses. 3. Its induction supports that bacterially induced RGI involves excess auxin signaling and is suppressed by Variovorax.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal degradation step of precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates ACC, lowering the ethylene precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Reduces precursor pool for ethylene-mediated root growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: CL14; MF160; B4; YR216 screened across phylogeny
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Presence/absence surveyed across Variovorax genomes; absent in YR216 which failed to suppress ACC-induced RGI
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ACC treatment inducing root growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential microbial trait for engineering/selection of plant-beneficial inoculants that mitigate ethylene-mediated root inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study did not functionally manipulate this gene directly; evidence is comparative genomics plus strain phenotype correlation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics across Variovorax phylogeny; physiological assay of strain ability to suppress ACC-induced RGI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Variovorax strains generally suppressed ACC-induced RGI. 2. Strain YR216 lacked ACC deaminase and did not suppress ACC-induced RGI. 3. This supports ACC deaminase as a likely contributor to ethylene-related growth rescue.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative genomics of Variovorax genomes; KEGG annotation scan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ethylene inhibits root elongation and acts synergistically with auxin in this system

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Putative auxin degradation operon enzymes including weak homologues of iacC, iacD and iacE
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Degradation route acting directly on target signal metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: A Variovorax-specific gene cluster degrades IAA, lowering exogenous auxin available to the plant-root system
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control locus for this phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Operon with weak homologues to Paraburkholderia iacC, iacD, iacE, iacF and iacR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible Lac promoter in pBBR1-MCS2 for vector V2 gain-of-function construct
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Acidovorax sp. root219; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hotspot 33 was the most highly upregulated Variovorax-specific hotspot in coculture with Arthrobacter CL28
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Coculture with RGI-inducing Arthrobacter CL28; exogenous IAA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAA degradation and suppresses auxin-associated root growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate locus for microbial engineering or transfer into beneficial rhizobacteria to buffer excess auxin and stabilize root development in complex microbiomes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Hotspot 33 contains weak homologues to known iac genes but lacks iacA, iacB and iacI of Paraburkholderia minimal set, indicating a distinct uncharacterized auxin catabolic system; V2 provided full rescue of IAA-induced RGI and partial rescue of Arthrobacter-induced RGI in Acidovorax; deletion mutant lost rescue without colonization defect
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: iacC; iacD; iacE; iacF; iacR homologues within hotspot 33
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; bacterial RNA-seq; genomic library screening in E. coli; gain-of-function in Acidovorax root219::V2; deletion mutant Variovorax CL14 ΔHS33; in vitro IAA degradation assay; plant root phenotype assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A Variovorax-specific hotspot 33 containing weak iac homologues was uniquely induced by coculture with Arthrobacter CL28. 2. Two genomic library clones containing hotspot 33 degraded IAA in E. coli. 3. Transfer of V2 into Acidovorax root219 conferred IAA degradation and RGI rescue activity. 4. Deletion of hotspot 33 in Variovorax CL14 abolished IAA degradation and suppression of IAA- and Arthrobacter-induced RGI. 5. Therefore hotspot 33 is the critical genetic locus underlying Variovorax maintenance of stereotypic root development.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative genomics across 185 genomes; Variovorax CL14 bacterial RNA-seq; plant RNA-seq context; NCBI SRA PRJNA543313; GEO GSE131158
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Acidovorax V2 only partially rescued Arthrobacter-induced RGI, likely due in part to lower colonization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: IAA is a plant hormone controlling root development; excess levels inhibit primary root growth
